mission.title: Install and Configure Dalec on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dalec is a project aimed at providing a declarative format for building system packages and containers with a focus on supply chain security. You would install it to produce secure packages and containers efficiently.

steps.title: Clone the Dalec repository
steps.description:
Start by cloning the Dalec repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/project-dalec/dalec.git
cd dalec
```

steps.title: Build the Dalec container image
steps.description:
Use Docker to build the Dalec container image from the Dockerfile provided in the repository:
```bash
docker build -t dalec:v0.20.3 .
```

steps.title: Run the Dalec container
steps.description:
Run the Dalec container to verify that it is working correctly:
```bash
docker run --rm dalec:v0.20.3 --help
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Dalec container by creating a Kubernetes deployment. First, create a deployment YAML file:
```yaml
yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: dalec
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: dalec
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: dalec
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: dalec
        image: dalec:v0.20.3
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
``` 
Then apply it using:
```bash
kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Dalec pod is running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Dalec deployment
uninstall.description:
Remove the Dalec deployment from your Kubernetes cluster:
```bash
kubectl delete deployment dalec
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Dalec container image
uninstall.description:
If you want to clean up the local Docker environment, remove the Dalec image:
```bash
docker rmi dalec:v0.20.3
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl get deployment dalec -o yaml > dalec-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Build the new version of Dalec
upgrade.description:
Build the new version of the Dalec container image:
```bash
docker build -t dalec:v0.20.3 .
```

upgrade.title: Update the deployment
upgrade.description:
Update the deployment to use the new image version:
```bash
kubectl set image deployment/dalec dalec=dalec:v0.20.3
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded Dalec pod is running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Dalec pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name>
``` 
Common issues may include missing dependencies or incorrect configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Image pull errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter image pull errors, ensure that the image name and tag are correct. You can check the available images with:
```bash
docker images
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits exceeded
troubleshooting.description:
If the pod is crashing due to resource limits, consider increasing the memory or CPU limits in the deployment YAML and redeploying:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment dalec
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Dalec pod running without errors, and you will be able to access its features through the container. Verify the pod status and logs to ensure everything is functioning correctly.
codeSnippets:
yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: dalec
spec:
  replicas: 1

    matchLabels:

      containers:
      - name: dalec
        image: dalec:v0.20.3
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
